title: Stemma Codicum of the Prajñāpāramitāhṛdaya

methodology.principle: Following Nattier (1992), the Heart Sutra was composed in Chinese ca. 649-656 CE, likely by or in the circle of Xuanzang. The Sanskrit text is a back-translation, not the original. This stemma reflects textual relationships, not chronological manuscript dates.
limitations: Some relationships are hypothetical based on textual analysis

hypothetical.name: Large Sutra Excerpts
hypothetical.description: Passages extracted from Kumārajīva's T223 Mahāprajñāpāramitā-sūtra (ca. 404 CE)

hypothetical.name: Proto-Heart Sutra (Chinese)
hypothetical.description: Hypothetical early compilation, possibly related to T250 tradition

hypothetical.name: Xuanzang's Xīnjīng
hypothetical.description: The Chinese Heart Sutra as composed/compiled, likely by Xuanzang ca. 649-656 CE

hypothetical.name: Sanskrit Back-Translation
hypothetical.description: First Sanskrit rendering from Chinese, created in China or Central Asia

hypothetical.name: Sanskrit Long Recension
hypothetical.description: Elaborated Sanskrit version with frame narrative added

hypothetical.name: Tantric Elaboration
hypothetical.description: Sanskrit tradition with Tantric additions (oṃ, Bhagavatī maṅgala)

chinese_witnesses.name: Dàmíngzhòujīng
chinese_witnesses.description: Possibly reflects pre-Xuanzang tradition or parallel development

chinese_witnesses.name: Fangshan Stele
chinese_witnesses.description: Oldest dated physical witness, derived from T251 tradition

chinese_witnesses.name: Tang Transliteration
chinese_witnesses.description: Bilingual text documenting Sanskrit as known in Tang China

chinese_witnesses.name: Dānapāla Translation
chinese_witnesses.description: Translation from elaborated Sanskrit long recension

sanskrit_witnesses.name: Hōryūji Manuscript
sanskrit_witnesses.description: Earliest Sanskrit manuscript witness, close to initial back-translation

sanskrit_witnesses.name: Nepalese MS Cambridge 1165
sanskrit_witnesses.description: Long recension with oṃ in mantra

tibetan_witnesses.name: Tibetan Long Recension
tibetan_witnesses.description: Translated from Sanskrit long recension with frame narrative

edges.note: Re-translation to Chinese from Tantric Sanskrit

frame_narrative.origin: Added to Sanskrit tradition (σ-L), probably 8th century

na_aprapti.origin: Interpolation in Sanskrit tradition, absent from earliest witnesses

scholarly_refs: Nattier, Jan (1992). 'The Heart Sūtra: a Chinese apocryphal text?' JIABS 15(2): 153-223.
Attwood, Jayarava (2017). 'Xuánzàng's Relationship to the Heart Sutra in Light of the Fangshan Stele.' JOCBS 12: 58-90.
Attwood, Jayarava (2019). 'The Buddhas of the Three Times and the Chinese Origins of the Heart Sutra.' JOCBS 16: 9-41.
Silk, Jonathan (1994). 'The Heart Sūtra in Tibetan: A Critical Edition of the Two Recensions.' WSTB 34.
Tanahashi, Kazuaki (2014). The Heart Sutra: A Comprehensive Guide. Shambhala.
